fr_loire_vacheron_sancerre_blanc.appearance: Pale lemon with green-gold highlights; brilliant clarity; light, watery tears suggesting moderate alcohol and dry extract
fr_loire_vacheron_sancerre_blanc.nose_summary: White peach, candied papaya, grapefruit zest and gunflint on the initial nose. Secondary notes of honeysuckle, sea spray, tarragon and subtle floral honeyed character. Restrained pyrazine expression -- more stone fruit and mineral than green/grassy. The silex-limestone-marl terroir blend delivers layered mineral complexity rather than overt varietal typicity.
fr_loire_vacheron_sancerre_blanc.palate_summary: Frank, full and mature entry with clean citrus-driven flavours of grapefruit and lemon. Flinty, stony mid-palate with piquant citrus fruitiness. Rounded but muscular texture -- not chiseled. Chalky, mineral-infused finish with saline persistence. Laser-sharp focus with good balance between ripe fruit and high acidity.
fr_loire_vacheron_sancerre_blanc.structural_summary: High acidity (vibrant, mouthwatering); no tannin; medium-minus body; 13-14% ABV well-integrated; dry; finish is medium-plus with lingering saline minerality and citrus peel

fr_loire_dagueneau_silex.appearance: Medium to deep lemon-gold, approaching straw; brilliant clarity; moderate legs/tears suggesting 13-13.5% ABV and textural weight from barrel fermentation
fr_loire_dagueneau_silex.nose_summary: Intense gunflint, struck match and smoky minerality dominate the opening. Dried yellow plum, dried apricot, kiwi fruit and passion fruit emerge with air. Robust grapefruit with bitter pith, white pepper, and a salty saline suggestion. Subtle oak polish beneath -- hints of cedar, vanilla, honeysuckle and bread dough. A fascinating combination of mineral intensity, tropical lift and restrained oak complexity. Develops green apple, fennel and chalk notes with extended aeration.
fr_loire_dagueneau_silex.palate_summary: Completely dry. Smooth, creamy textured entry with intense concentration. Kiwi fruit, passion fruit, stone fruit and apricot poking through the creamy core. Forceful acidity brings salty, mineral precision to the mid-palate. Bitter citrus (grapefruit) and white fruit (apple, plum) drive the palate. Subtle cedar and vanilla notes from oak are impeccably integrated. Long, mineral-laced finish with saline persistence. Demands decanting -- opens dramatically over 2+ hours.
fr_loire_dagueneau_silex.structural_summary: High acidity (crisp, salivating, masked by fruit concentration); no tannin; medium body with notable textural weight from lees and barrel work; 13-13.5% ABV; bone dry; very long finish with building mineral intensity

fr_loire_huet_le_mont_sec.appearance: Pale gold to light golden with silver-green highlights in youth; brilliant clarity; light legs consistent with 13% ABV dry style
fr_loire_huet_le_mont_sec.nose_summary: Taut and precise: citrus (lemon, green apple), quince and white peach layered over smoky, flinty minerality. Crushed shell and saline notes form a mineral backbone. With age, develops honeyed notes, nuts, lanolin, pear and dried fruit character. The Le Mont site brings a reticent, flinty character that develops the strongest perfume with bottle age -- initially less charming than other Huet cuvees but ultimately the most complex.
fr_loire_huet_le_mont_sec.palate_summary: Taut and dry with crystalline purity. Peach, quince and orange peel with underlying mineral depth. Medium-bodied with a slightly viscous, smooth texture despite the linearity. Clean saline finish with lingering citrus peel and wet stone. Well-balanced acidity that retains freshness even at elevated ripeness levels. Stops the drinker in their tracks with depth and concentration. Light-bodied yet concentrated in warm vintages (2023); steely and flinty in cooler years.
fr_loire_huet_le_mont_sec.structural_summary: High acidity (malic acid dominant -- no MLF encouraged, contributing to age-worthiness); no tannin; medium-minus to medium body; 13% ABV; dry (though residual sugar can vary slightly by vintage); long clean finish with mineral persistence; 10-20 year aging potential

fr_loire_closel_clos_du_papillon.appearance: Slightly golden-tinged to deep gold in colour (deeper than Vouvray); brilliant clarity; moderate viscosity suggesting richer body and higher extract from late-harvest fruit and extended lees ageing
fr_loire_closel_clos_du_papillon.nose_summary: Beeswax, roasted almonds, golden apple and mirabelle plum with apricot and quince. Lifted by acacia honey and floral notes. Herbal, minerally complexity with savoury intensity -- tight and firm. Flinty, chalky notes with green apple and orange rind. Waxy, straw-like character typical of Savennieres Chenin. Creamy oak undertone from 420L demi-muids. Pronounced schist/volcanic mineral signature distinct from Vouvray's tuffeau.
fr_loire_closel_clos_du_papillon.palate_summary: Dry, mid-weight and intensely structured. Full-bodied with generous richness and powerful slate-driven minerality on the finish. Firm lemony acidity frames the concentrated palate. Mealy, herby, waxy flavours with quince, apricot and a distinguished touch of bitterness or toasted almonds on the finish. The long lees ageing gives a smooth, rounded texture, but the schist terroir maintains savoury tension and taut structure. More imposing and savoury than Vouvray -- classic Savennieres intensity.
fr_loire_closel_clos_du_papillon.structural_summary: High acidity (racy, bracing -- Chenin's natural high acid preserved through biodynamic farming and earlier picking); no tannin; medium-plus body; 13.5-14% ABV (can reach 14.5-15% in riper vintages); dry; very long finish with incredible length and slate mineral persistence; 15-25 year aging potential

za_swartland_mullineux_granite_chenin.appearance: Pale straw to light gold; brilliant clarity; light to moderate tears consistent with 13.5% ABV; slight oily texture visible on the glass
za_swartland_mullineux_granite_chenin.nose_summary: Restrained and mineral-driven: real mineral saline note over apple, pear and slight citric lemon. Toasty, gunpowdery/gunflint notes from indigenous yeast fermentation (not new oak). Bright fruit flavours -- green apple, pear skin, crushed stone and Meyer lemon. Citrus blossom emerges with air. Stone fruit undertones. The granite terroir delivers a distinctly flinty, aromatic character -- more aromatic than the Schist or Iron cuvees due to fruit ripening in a cooler micro-environment.
za_swartland_mullineux_granite_chenin.palate_summary: Absolutely bone dry with mouthwatering acidity. Very precise with a taut, linear delivery. Medium to full-bodied with delightful mineral tension. Dusty pears, yellow and green apples, fresh-cut Meyer lemon. Sapid saline note on the finish with good length. Slight oily texture adds roundedness to the linear frame. Toasty complexity from indigenous fermentation, not oak. Cerebral wine that keeps you thinking -- gliding to a fresh, mineral-laced finish. Food-friendly for 20+ years.
za_swartland_mullineux_granite_chenin.structural_summary: High acidity (striking vertical freshness -- highest natural acidity of the three Mullineux Chenin cuvees due to deep-rooted vines in granite); no tannin; medium to full body; 13.5% ABV; bone dry (RS 1.8 g/l in 2024); long finish with salty mineral persistence; 20+ year aging potential

it_barolo_gd_vajra_albe.appearance: Medium ruby to garnet with a lively, pigeon-blood core; classic pale Nebbiolo translucency; moderate tears consistent with 14% ABV
it_barolo_gd_vajra_albe.nose_summary: Bright and expressive: fresh cherries, red currant, macerated strawberries and raspberry. Crushed dried flowers, rose petal, violet and hawthorn florals. Interlaced with mint, lavender, rosehip, rhubarb and licorice. Hints of red fig and orange peel. With age, develops earthy, dusty notes with a slight balsamic quality. Sweet spices emerge alongside crushed stone and granite minerality. Aromatic and perfumed -- varietal authenticity is a signature of this high-altitude cuvee.
it_barolo_gd_vajra_albe.palate_summary: Generous, layered and youthful entry. Vibrant red fruit core -- marasca cherry, raspberry and red currant underscored by vibrant acidity. Harmonious and supple in texture, with peppery strawberry and crushed allspice notes. Iron and juniper give savoury complexity. Ripe but refined tannins frame a silky, multi-layered mid-palate. Classic finish on tones of marasca cherry, mineral and floral elements. Already irresistible in good vintages but built for moderate bottle ageing.
it_barolo_gd_vajra_albe.structural_summary: High acidity (vibrant, energetic); high tannin (fine, velvet, ripe -- gripping but not aggressive); medium body; 14% ABV; dry; long aftertaste of red fruit, mineral and floral; best from release through 20+ years; 18-26 months in large Slavonian oak casks (neutral)

it_barbaresco_produttori_del_barbaresco.appearance: Pale garnet to medium ruby; classic Nebbiolo translucency with slight orange-tinged rim on older vintages; clean and bright; moderate tears
it_barbaresco_produttori_del_barbaresco.nose_summary: Rose, cherry, strawberry and violet florals. White pepper, dried cranberry and raspberry with cassis undertones. Leather, smoke, tobacco and cedar add complexity. Lavender, fig, balsamic, dried leaves and wet stone. Classic tar-and-roses Barbaresco character. In warmer vintages (2018), darker fruit with truffle, licorice, grilled herb and raisin/dried apricot. More perfumed and immediately aromatic than Barolo -- the cooperative blend captures the essence of multiple Barbaresco terroirs.
it_barbaresco_produttori_del_barbaresco.palate_summary: Vibrant and well-defined. Rose, cherry and strawberry fruit core with white pepper and mineral flavours. Sleek and elegant with firm, dusty yet ripe tannins. Smoky and juicy mid-palate. Medium to full body with good concentration. Long savory finish with mineral earth and fennel/thyme notes. Balanced and approachable in youth yet structured for ageing. Linear finish that firms up with excellent length. Classic scents of tar and roses carry through to the palate.
it_barbaresco_produttori_del_barbaresco.structural_summary: High acidity (vibrant, fresh); high tannin (firm, dusty, well-integrated -- slightly softer than Barolo); medium to full body; 14% ABV; dry; long linear finish with excellent length; 24+ months in large botti (2,500-5,000L); best from 5 years after vintage through 20+ years

it_montalcino_il_poggione_brunello.appearance: Ruby red with good depth and youthful intensity; saturated and vibrant in the glass with no sign of evolution; good full ruby-red colour with darker concentration than many peers in warm vintages
it_montalcino_il_poggione_brunello.nose_summary: Dark, earthy and complex: crushed ashen stone, rosemary, cedar and exotic spice open the bouquet. Core of raspberry preserves and black cherry. Violet inner florals, leather, graphite and tobacco weave through. Dried fruit and autumnal forest floor, dark licorice, wet slate, cola and grilled rosemary on second nose. Luscious red fruit (red plum, pomegranate) with outstanding mineral lift. Structured and layered rather than immediately expressive -- rewards patience. Roses washed in mulberry, cinnamon-spiced pomegranate and jasmine/tea leaf notes in top vintages.
it_montalcino_il_poggione_brunello.palate_summary: Warm and balanced with velvet-smooth tannins. Silky texture that flows across the palate, building in tension. Tart wild berries and orange hints give way to a sweet herbal thrust. Dense but weightless and sappy with distinctly juicy fruitiness. Energizing minerality to fine-grained fruit and floral flavours. Plum, cherry, iron, tobacco and underbrush flavours buoyed by a plump texture. Firm, crunchy tannins that are powdery and dry. Tremendous length with classic structure. The 15% ABV is felt but integrated.
it_montalcino_il_poggione_brunello.structural_summary: High acidity (vibrant, focusing the dense structure); high tannin (powdery, dry, crunchy -- firm and youthful, needing time); full body; 15% ABV (warm, noticeable but integrated); dry; very long finish with building noble tannins and mineral persistence; best from 5-7 years after vintage through 25+ years; aged in large French botti (30-50 hl)

it_chianti_classico_felsina_rancia_riser.appearance: Ruby red with rich, intense tones; medium-deep with garnet highlights; classic Sangiovese translucency at the rim; clean and bright
it_chianti_classico_felsina_rancia_riser.nose_summary: Spicy nose with floral notes (violet) and hints of wild berry -- both red (Marasca cherry, raspberry) and black (black currant). Mineral impressions with crushed limestone/flint signature. Dried herb, balsamic, eucalyptus and new leather accents. Light toasted notes from French barrique ageing. Earth, tobacco and white truffle with tilled earth complexity. Wild cherry and raspberry dominate in youth; develops secondary complexity of incense, dried leaves and menthol with age. The dusty minerality is the wine's special signature.
it_chianti_classico_felsina_rancia_riser.palate_summary: Spice and crisp fruit on entry with self-confident but supple tannins. Impressive structure and breed. Ripe Marasca cherry and plum with brown spice, exotic incense and a minty finish. Beautiful minerality with dusty, crushed limestone or flint notes. Taut and linear, with elegant delivery. Firm tannins frame the elements, imparting structure and complexity for the long haul. Terrific texture -- silky in warm years (2018), more structured and stubborn in classic vintages (2016). The 100% Sangiovese purity shows through with a sour cherry, dried herb and tobacco core.
it_chianti_classico_felsina_rancia_riser.structural_summary: High acidity (fresh, balancing -- signature of the Castelnuovo Berardenga zone at 400-420m altitude); medium-plus tannin (firm but supple, from French barriques aged 16-20 months); medium-plus body; 14% ABV; dry; vigorous and taut finish with supporting acidity; best from 5-10 years after vintage through 20+ years; needs patience -- hits prime at 15-20 years
